name: ABBV-CLS-628
mechanism: Undisclosed mechanism; IV infusion every 4 weeks for 92 weeks
notes: Multicenter across ~100 sites in 11 countries. Primary endpoint: Total Kidney Volume change via MRI at Week 96.

name: AL01211
notes: Phase 1 in healthy volunteers completed June 2022. No Phase 2 registered yet.

name: Atorvastatin
mechanism: Statin combined with acidosis correction to slow CKD progression in ADPKD
notes: Open-label pilot study testing atorvastatin with or without sodium bicarbonate.

name: AZD1613
mechanism: PAPPA-1 inhibitor — reduces local IGF signaling that drives cyst growth
notes: Novel mechanism. SC/IV multiple ascending dose study. Sites in US, UK, China.

name: Bempedoic Acid
mechanism: ACLY inhibitor / AMPK activator — FDA-approved for cholesterol, pro-drug activated in kidney/liver
notes: Preclinical data showed cyst growth inhibition in ADPKD models. 180mg daily for 2 years.

name: Dapagliflozin
mechanism: SGLT2 inhibitor — first dedicated Phase 3 in ADPKD
notes: 10mg daily for 36 months vs placebo. Primary endpoint: chronic eGFR slope. Sites across Germany, Netherlands, Austria, Spain.

name: Empagliflozin
mechanism: SGLT2 inhibitor — testing safety in ADPKD with or without tolvaptan co-administration
notes: Three parallel trials: EMPA-PKD (NCT06391450, Hannover, n=44), SIDIA (NCT06435858, Zurich, n=40, recruiting), Colorado Feasibility (NCT05510115, n=50).

name: Farabursen (CYX082)
mechanism: Anti-miR-17 oligonucleotide — inhibits microRNA-17, restoring polycystin-1/2 protein production to reduce cyst growth. 300mg SC every 2 weeks.
notes: Phase 1b showed htTKV growth halted (0.05% vs 2.58% placebo). Novartis acquired Regulus for $1.7B (closed Jun 2025). Phase 3 registrational trial planned; data presented at ASN Kidney Week 2025 and WCN 2026.

name: JMKX003142
mechanism: Oral small molecule — mechanism not publicly disclosed. Targets TKV reduction in rapidly progressive ADPKD.
notes: Multiple Phase 1 PK/DDI studies also ongoing.

name: Metformin
mechanism: AMPK activator — inhibits CFTR (reduces cyst fluid secretion) and indirectly inhibits mTOR pathway
notes: Largest PKD trial to date. Prior Phase 2 (TAME-PKD) showed safety and encouraging trend. Up to 2000mg daily.

name: Probenecid
mechanism: Reduces tolvaptan-induced polyuria — makes tolvaptan therapy more tolerable
notes: Study completed. Aimed to improve urine osmolality and reduce excessive urination from tolvaptan.

name: PYC-003
mechanism: Peptide-PMO conjugate antisense therapy targeting PKD1 mutations, delivered IV
notes: Three-part study: healthy volunteer SAD, then ADPKD patient SAD and MAD. Sites in Australia/New Zealand.

name: Rotigotine
mechanism: Dopamine D5 receptor agonist (transdermal patch) — restores endothelial mechanosensitivity and NO release
notes: Prior IMPROVE-PKD study showed beneficial effects over 2 months. Addresses defective polycystin-mediated mechanotransduction.

name: Tamibarotene
mechanism: Synthetic retinoid (retinoic acid receptor agonist) — modulates renal cell proliferation/differentiation
notes: Targets patients unable/unwilling to take tolvaptan. 52-week treatment. 8 sites in Japan.

name: Tirzepatide
mechanism: GLP-1/GIP dual receptor agonist — targets visceral adiposity, insulin resistance, and kidney inflammation
notes: With Washington University and Mayo Clinic (NIDDK/NIH). Restricted to overweight/obese ADPKD patients (BMI ≥ 27).

name: VX-407
mechanism: Oral small molecule — likely polycystin-1 modulator/corrector for specific PKD1 gene variants
notes: Precision medicine approach analogous to Vertex's CFTR modulators for cystic fibrosis. Phase 1 (159 participants) fully completed.

name: Tolvaptan (Jynarque)
mechanism: Vasopressin V2 receptor antagonist — blocks cAMP-driven cyst fluid secretion and cell proliferation
notes: FDA approved April 2018 for ADPKD. The only drug specifically approved for PKD. Requires liver monitoring (REMS program). Causes significant polyuria (4–8L/day urine).
